name: hypromellose
description: Hypromellose is a cellulose-derived polymer used as a thickener in food products to improve texture and consistency.
raw_text: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose (Explained + Products) Products Ingredients Decode INCI Login Register Login Register Products Ingredients Decode INCI Follow us on our new Insta page » Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose What-it-does: surfactant/cleansing , viscosity controlling , emulsion stabilising Irritancy: 0 Comedogenicity: 1 Official CosIng Information All Functions: antistatic, binding, emulsion stabilising, film forming, surfactant, viscosity controlling Description: cellulose, 2-hydroxypropyl methyl ether CAS #: 9004-65-3 Ph. Eur. Name: Methylhydroxypropylcellulosum Details A handy helper ingredient (a polymer, i.e. big molecule from repeated subunits) that is used to stabilize emulsions as well as to thicken up products . It can also stabilize foam in cleansing products. [more] [more] [less] Something incorrect or missing? Let us know!
